title: arctostaphylos pungens subsp. hispidula
intro: Arctostaphylos pungens subsp. hispidula, also known as Bigberry Manzanita, is a subspecies of evergreen shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Bigberry Manzanita have been shown to provide various health benefits, including cardiovascular protection, immune system modulation, and antimicrobial activity. Bigberry Manzanita is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed as a dried leaf or berry supplement.
